reviews.review_description: I like the game but can you add more combat things in it. There is only endless mode and 1 world which is quite short.
I love how you go from fighting then taking a break while you make food. Its both fun and challenging

The game was born from the ashes of the Red Flowers demo, which serves as the foundation for this title.
The demo is inspired by Ghostrunner, a ninja parkour game (you run here while shaking hands, jump, wall-run, use a grappling hook, dash, etc.).
Therefore, I would advise against playing the game for people prone to motion sickness.

Catana takes the basics and makes the game more colorful and features a cat as the main character.
The game has no story and is contextualized by a cutscene.

The majority of the game (80% ) focuses on two main closely linked activities which earn coins : 
- An Endless mode with a leaderboard (with adjustable difficulty/modifiers) reminiscent of a mobile game.
Interesting because it uses the gameplay of Red Flowers, but it quickly becomes tedious and boring, therefore not very engaging.
- A restaurant mini-game: fairly solid and basic, but quite uninteresting due to its virtually nonexistent difficulty combined with almost no constraints.

The coins earned are used to unlock activities in the restaurant and cosmetic items.
The core game design resembles a checklist that requires you to complete repetitive tasks with the sole incentive being to unlock everything. 
There are also achievements that reinforce this principle.

The game is paradoxically saved by a bonus mode (Time Attack), which is a remake of the Red Flowers demo.
It consists of ~10-11 hand-crafted linear levels where you have to get from point A to point B as quickly as possible while using parkour and eliminating designated targets.
It plays very well, even if it's ultimately quite short. 
The mode's lifespan is extended if you're aiming for the gold medal (which demands flawless precision and speed) and the presence of a leaderboard might motivate the most competitive players.

A major disappointment, then, despite initial curiosity: the game tries to appeal to a wider audience by mixing trendy elements , but this results in a product that blows hot and cold. A shame.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed review and for taking the time to break everything down.

The Time Run levels were brought over from the Red Flowers demo deliberately, and getting strong times across all of them takes a lot of practice and mastery.

As for the restaurant, yes, it’s different in tone by design. It’s meant to be a lower-pressure space for breaks between intense runs, not another combat or challenge-heavy mode.

We understand that different parts of the game will resonate with different players, and that contrast was intentional to let people focus on what they enjoy most, or try something new.

reviews.review_description:
Please read!!!

 Gameplay & Mechanics: The game features high-speed parkour, a dash ability, and a grappling hook/rope for traversal. You play as a customizable kitten, with options for cosmetics.

 Cooking & Modes: It includes a relaxing cooking/bistro mode, along with an "endless mode," "time trial mode," and global leaderboards. Interaction: 

A key feature is messing around with NPCs Especially grandpa. 

No In-App Purchases: The game is described as being focused on gameplay and in-game currency for cosmetics,And rather than forced purchases. 

Kitty: You play as a lil kitty who can meow with right thumbstick and have little kitty hands.

reviews.review_title: Great to help out testing this game
reviews.review_description:
Yep strange game playing as a cat in two modes a stride mode but some could love the cat mode to move to.
you can play a infinity mode where you jump around islands to level up
but for the real striders there also the real red flowers part as a time trial find the table and place your pawn on the table to go to a secret place of the older days

Catana seems like it doesn't know what it's trying to be.  The cutesy vibrant style clashes with the blood and gore of killing, but then you are unable to actually harm the characters in the village.  Between the mobile endless runner gameplay and the food service prep it feels like two unrelated and unpolished janky games awkwardly combined.  There are only about 10 actually curated levels most of which last a matter of seconds.  Things often feel frustratingly glitchy and unreliable such as the dash attack, grappling, and wall running.  This could have been a great game but it needs more work and to be taken in a better direction than just chasing trends.

developer_response.body:
We understand why that contrast stands out.
In the restaurant, you can’t harm guests on purpose, that’s intentional. That space is meant to be about cooking, playful interactions, and taking a breather from high-intensity runs, not combat or violence.

The restaurant is also directly tied to progression: Endless modes let you earn resources that feed back into the restaurant, and the restaurant itself has its own upgrade and progression systems.

The Time Run levels were brought over from the original demo deliberately, as a nod to long-time Red Flowers players. And we’re confident that getting strong times across all of them takes a lot of practice and mastery.

As for the movement and combat mechanics, if something felt unreliable, we’d genuinely like to know more details. A lot of testing (including open tests) went into these systems before release, and they’re very timing and precision-based, which can take some time to get used to for new players.

If you’re open to it, feel free to share more specifics in our Discord, detailed feedback really helps.

reviews.review_description:
Catana: Red Flowers is a wild Mix of 2 very different Games. 
First you got a Katana-Parkour-Speedrun-Game, very similiar to Flat-Games like Ghostrunner.
 Theres multiple Stages to beat. You also get an Endless Mode, with Leaderboards and all. This whole Ghostrunner-like Side of the Game is an absolute Blast. 
And don't be fooled by the cute Artstyle, as this Gamemode got endless Gore, as you can slice Enemies in Half and such, very much in Contrast to the other Game-Mode.
The Movement is fun, fluid and engaging, the Visuals are sharp and Performance is smooth. 
I just wished there were more Levels, as you can beat them very quickly. 
Maybe a future DLC can add some more. Would be highly appreciated.

On the other Side of the Game you get a Restaurant-Cooking-Game, where you prepare Meals and serve them to hungry Customers. Its well made, has good Animations and funny Interactions. But i gotta say, it's an odd Choice to put those two vastly different Games into One Package. Maybe the Dev should've released them seperated, but as the Price of this Game is so low, i can't complain at all.
So whether you like Action Games like Stride or Ghostrunner, or are a Fan of Restaurant-Games that don't take themselves too serious, i'd highly recommend this little Gem, especiallyfor that amazing Price!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the thoughtful review. Really glad the parkour side, movement, and performance clicked for you. We hear the feedback about wanting more levels, and appreciate you taking the time to break down both sides of the game so clearly.

reviews.review_title: Good game but a little buggy on Quest 2
reviews.review_description: The overall gameplay felt quite nice to play, the tutorial also did a excellent job teaching me what I needed to know to play the game. The options of either parkour movement (the one I chose) or cat movement (like gorilla tag) is great because assuming someone likes gorilla tag movement will absolutely disappoint players like me who doesn't like gorilla tag movement and assuming everyone will like the parkour movement will disappoint players who want to feel more like a cat while playing a game about a cat. Overall great game but the bugs really broke my immersion especially the bug where the camera movement just moved on it's own disorienting me and leaving me lost unsure of what side I have to go to now is really annoying, other than that the game was quite fun.

Okay now that I've got your attention, this is a fast pace parkour ninja style of game that incorporates high intensity quick responses and reflex along side cooking and restaurant management. This game is very comical in the cooking aspect while being high intensity for the parkour and ninja aspect. This game lets you run around as a cat doing things you can only imagine a cat could do as a ninja. Fun game lots of laughs a lot of really cool upgrades gear and restaurant building. If you ever enjoyed any cooking game or any parkour game this is a delightful mix of the two together definitely a lot of fun and worth owning whether you're into parkour or not you'll find this game worth your time and money. Plus it doesn't hurt to have a development team that actually listens to its players.

This game is an interesting mix of genres of parkour levels and more relaxing area building up a restaurant and making food items for guests.  In the parkour levels it gets you moving and jumping and flinging through levels similar to the Stride games. This is definitely a good thing and makes a nice work out moving through the levels.  I could do these levels all day as it gets me in the zone and into a good flow state.  If you get tired of these the other cozy restaurant section you could work on building as you gain more money to grow it.  I would love if we could have a more open world area in the future maybe being able to stransport food across the city before the time runs out. It could be called a KittyDash service.  If you loved the other games by this studio do yourself a favor and try this one out too!

Catana plays very similar to those endless runner type mobile games but instead of swiping your fingers you are using your whole body to run, leap, grapple, deflect bullets, and slice down enemies. By collecting coins in the endless runner mode or by running your mini shop, you can purchase cosmetic upgrades. The game is entertaining but can become a little repetitive. I would like to see a little more  map variation and maybe different weapon types to mix things up.

I recently sat down with the bright and colorful Catana Red Flowers and found it to be an interesting mix of parkour-type action and an idle like cooking section. There is also a throwaway fishing pond, but that wasn't very fleshed out. Unfortunately for an in-production game, I found the control lacking. It begins with a lackluster tutorial that doesn't really explain much more than the basics of movement (the jump button delay and overall scheme were a hurdle for me). After that you are put in a hub section where movement is free, and you can play a cooking game, which I found quite entertaining. But again, after a while of cooking, the stove just stopped working, and I couldn't just close the store and go back to either endless or timed action levels. I stopped playing in frustration, which is unfortunate since I think there is a fun game hidden beneath the bugs that I hope will be ironed out. For me, a 2.5/10. (version 0.18.051)

A charming yet somewhat jarring amalgamation of restaurant management and parkour/ninja themed time trials. Although I enjoyed my time with this title, the mix of art styles and disparate gameplay mechanics means Catana has trouble establishing a solid identity.  Hoping to see further refinement to really make this one purr 😺

I love the fast-paced nature of this game and the fact that I can do different game modes is awesome. I like running the restaurants and the Infinity level and the individual levels. It has some really great modifiers that you can use during your runs. The game is very well made and the visuals are pretty. It has a very polished feel to the game.

I initially experienced motion sickness while playing this game, but I switched to another game for a while and then came back to it. After playing for a bit, the motion sickness went away.

The game is very fun and fast-paced, especially the combat. There’s a lot of movement and dashing. There are certain parts of the game where I’m unsure where I’m supposed to go. There should be an indicator showing where you need to go.

Other than that, I highly recommend playing this game.

reviews.review_title: Not what I was expecting & needs polish
reviews.review_description:
This game has 3 parts and none have any story campaign structure.

I think the main mode is actually this Endless mode where you pick difficulty, modifiers that you have to buy to unlock, using purchased equipment that is either permanent or consumable unlock for your next run, and option to buy cosmetic unlocks for your sword and gloves. You earn points as you pick up coins and other stuff and maybe slicing dudes or just advancing?

There is an additional mode you can get to from hub area for short time trial levels that require you to complete levels within very tight timeline that get progressively difficult. You earn points as you try and retry.

The third is a restaurant within the hub area that you can open and then you start getting customers that you have to cook food based on icon showing where they stand as a timer goes down. Deliver the correct foods on time to get points. The controls feel janky and the progression system grindy.

The game starts with an adequate tutorial that teaches you the basics of what you need for the Endless and Time Trial modes. The controls feel better for the Endless mode than Time Trial where faster times and more precise execution is required.

The game provides no tutorial for the restaurant part of game or what you do in hub area or how you fish or what the potions are for.

None of the leaderboards are functional. I never saw my own placement in any of them, I can't see friends, I can't see local. There are no filters or ways to make the leaderboards be meaningful.

I love the theme of being a ninja cat, and combining a precision speedrunning styled game with a more-chill food prepping and serving game is very unique. However, I have experienced some control issues with the game (attacks occurring twice, when I have only physically attacked once, for example). I also have to note that I did experience some slight vr motion sickness after playing this game after an extended play session (over an hour), as a result, I would hesitate to recommend this to someone new to VR or are prone to motion sickness.  I have submitted feedback to the developers, so hopefully things can be smoothed out with patches. Overall, this game has the ability to deliver the "one more run" and "that string of moved felt so fun" moments that games like Ghostrunner are known for. I didn't know I needed a ninja cat vr game in my collection before I saw this, but I am happy to have it now. I would give it a 3.75/5

reviews.review_title: Doesn’t know what it wants to be
reviews.review_description: The core gameplay is solid, a quick action game with store management. But these two just don’t mix very well, the story just doesn’t make sense to connect these two game genres. The endless mode doesn’t have enough tile sets to keep playing for an extended period, and the story missions aren’t really storylines. The game essentially has you play for purchasable cosmetics, there’s just no satisfaction in playing.

reviews.review_title: Solid enough for still being worked on
reviews.review_description: A very unique game with multiple combinations of styles. First you have the parkour which reminds me more of stride as you have the grapple, the sword in replacement of the gum, and the wall and jump functions. Then you have a restaurant game where you can do all sorts of stuff as well as the part where when making the fish foods you play a mini fruit ninja. And finally you the gorilla tag aspect IF you pick that mode of mobility. The environments are amazing for how simplistic it is as well as the models. I will say it might be slightly repetitive but i still recommend the game

A really fun collection of games. The main part is the platforming and the associated upgrades and cosmetics. They're really fun. 

The game has couple of things to improve on. Namely the running and dashing mechanics. Remove some weirdly frustrating character. And a bit more forgiving roping. 

The mini games were fun and a good break from all the swinging and slashing. 

Overall the collection of games delivers on the fun.

reviews.review_title: Fun with great movement and mechanics
reviews.review_description: At first I thought it was going to be another gorilla tag like game and while the movement is similar that's where the comparison ends the game is smooth and fluid you really feel immersed in the world it has that classic Japanese city skape and fast paced movement and combat but that's not all if you want something a bit more leisurely while still being fast you can run your resteraunt and serve food to customers with mini games to make the food all in all it's a very fun game with alot of veriety and and differences to make it a stand out game in the genre

reviews.review_title: The one of the best games of all time I
reviews.review_description:
I love the game. It reminds me of stride and I loved playing stride because the grappling and all that just feels so clean the wall running top and when the snipers shoot you and you just block it with your Cortana Top Gear I just really love it because it reminds me of plain stride.


￼

Catana Red Flowers is a game that anyone familiar with Stride would immediately be able to pickup and play.  However, I found the movement to be far better than Stride's.  Running, jumping, dashing, and sliding through the levels to slaughter the enemies feels pretty satisfying and the maps are laid out well.  While there's an endless mode, there's also levels you can go through with tailored maps.  If you like ninjas, cats, or Stride, don't skip this one!

reviews.review_title: Jack of all trades the master of some.
reviews.review_description:
Catana is a speedrunning game where you're a ninja cat with a katana and grappling hook trying to run a restaurant. Sounds crazy, right? Well, this game reminds me of multiple genres mashed into one VR title. The speedrunning aspect reminds me of Neon White, with levels having multiple ranks and the ability to race yourself again and again for the campaign. I wish there were hints about which direction you're meant to be going for the levels since it takes place on city rooftops but feels like an open world, which can lead to great shortcuts, but I need to know where I'm heading before trying to take a shortcut.

However, the main way to actually progress is by doing an endless mode where you're just running straight forward, which reminds me of Stride, where you're trying to run away from the fire (I'm guessing since it seems to be something burning while you're running). And the endless mode is just a couple of stages reused over and over, giving the effect of a loop, but after a while, you can guess what will come next. What if you procedurally generated the endless mode so it would be different every time?

You get coins for the restaurant to open up minigame-like cooking props, like a bamboo cup one where you have to cut bamboo either straight or sideways, and the more accurately you cut, the more cups you get. Cooking is also a minigame, involving a quick-time event on the grill. The fishing in the game is also inconsistent, as you're not able to throw the fishing rod where you want to catch a fish. I think it's meant to be designed by pressing the trigger just to drop the bait in the pond, but I would love to see the ability to throw it in like in real life and go a bit further into the pond.

The gameplay aspect, like the grappling hook, seems to be inconsistent. For example, I grapple towards a lamp, but I don't grapple towards an enemy; instead, I hook him towards me. If you want to "grapple" towards the enemy, that's what the dash is used for—to dash to them. Sometimes enemies may have a barrier that you either dash to get rid of or grapple to get rid of.

I would rather grappling be consistent and allow me to grapple to enemies because I keep dashing too far, and when I try to dash again, it's on cooldown. So, if grappling worked as I suggested, you could basically do a double dash towards the enemy, providing two options for getting closer to an enemy. I also wished that when an enemy shoots at you, instead of holding the katana to protect yourself, you would have to move the blade forward to try and deflect it to add more spice to the gameplay.

There's a lot of small bugs, like plates crashing on your katana handle, the fishing rod in general, and the cooking meter, as well as the bamboo slicing, and running mechanic. At first I liked all of those little mechanics between combat runs, but then I just found out how badly constructed they are. The game never explains how to use the fishing rod, and after I figured it out I still couldn't catch fish even though I perfectly timed my pulls with the meter mini game, and after I somehow caught a fish, it didn't give me anything, not even some money. Speaking of money, you can get it a lot faster by just beating down random people, which makes the store kinda useless, besides the fact that I still can't see how reputation helps me. Furthermore, I bought two other food mechanics, but then I found out that my hard work to get them only gives me less reputation and less money than the original mechanic, which makes me wonder why even bother the extra work, and progression is just useless and unrewarding. Also, the bamboo cutting mini game is super underwhelming, because the game doesn't register fast movements, so you just kind of follow the arrows slowly instead of actually cutting bamboo, which is super annoying and disappointing, especially because doing it doesn't reward you with more money or reputation when you serve the bamboo cups. The cooking meter is also buggy and isn't synchronized between each eye, so it just hurts my eyes to look at, which is such a basic thing to fix. Customers also don't do anything if you just don't serve them, which makes the entire cooking concept kind of underwhelming, since you're not under any real pressure. The actual combat is nice, but I hate how you lose all momentum by jumping or walking: you gain momentum by sliding, using the grapple, and slicing enemies, just to lose it when you jump for no reason. It's also super annoying that you have to move your hands all the time to run, and it gets kind of old really fast and is just ruining the flow and the fun of it. Also, nearly all the levels are the exact same as they were in the demo, on top of there being very little levels that you can finish in two hours or less. It's really weird because the game gives you a lot of things to purchase just for there to not be that many levels to use them in. Yes there is an endless mode, but I'm not gonna grind punching people for money just to buy a skin for an endless mode. I just feel like the game becomes very useless to play after finishing the campaign. So to summarize, I really hope they improve on it, cuz right now it just feels very unfinished. All in all, I still kinda liked the combat, so it's not horrible.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks a lot for taking the time to write such a detailed breakdown. There’s a lot here, and we’ll definitely go through the points you described.

Just to clarify a couple of things: customers in the restaurant do have individual timers once they place an order, even if the pressure is intentionally kept lower compared to the combat side.

As for the hand-swing movement, that’s one of the core locomotion ideas we use across several of our games. It can feel demanding at first, but once it clicks, it becomes part of the flow, and for many players it also works as a light fitness element.

Regarding Time Runs: there aren’t many of them, that’s true, but mastering them and pushing for strong times is meant to take significantly more practice if you choose to engage with that side of the game.

Thanks again for sharing such in-depth feedback. It’s genuinely helpful.

reviews.review_description:
a fun little game about playing as a cat cutting down enemies with your katana and grapple hook. It has an endless mode and 11 levels that are quite fun but only take up time if you're going for gold. The main hub where you select levels has a cooking mini game which is not bad but seems a bit out of place in the game. in general if you want to try a game like ghost runner but in vr play the levels and try the endless mode but don't expect much more than that. There are unlockables and most are easy to get or take a small grind but some things will need a lot of play time to unlock. 

Be prepared for motion sickness. This is a very fast movement game and it's easy to get sick. Lastly the combat is very simple while it can be fun it does get repetitive. no real bugs but when getting shot at you have to hit the bullet with the katana no matter how fast you are moving the bullet will hit you if not blocked.

I found this game particularly interesting, because I enjoyed playing Ghost runner. 
I like it, that it's a more family friendly game, which could be seen by its art style. The levels feel unique. And interesting, but sometimes it's hard to figure out where you're supposed to go.
The game is definitely not a good pick for someone new to VR, because it almost gave me motion sickness, and I'm highly resistant to that.
That said, It is a fun experience.

reviews.review_title: If it wasn’t broken, it would be better
reviews.review_description:
There is movement: running, button jump, slide, wallrun, hook, climbing and free climbing (on separate buttons). The movement is similar to Stride, but in many aspects it feels worse - less convenient and less polished.

Combat is extremely simple and has no depth at all: you hold the katana to block bullets, point it at an enemy, press the trigger and dash into them. You dont even really need to move your hand.

There are a lot of bugs. I was running into one every 2–3 minutes, and its very obvious these are bugs, not features.

The cafe area is generally okay: nice fish-mans, you can mess around a bit. It would be much better if it actually worked. Instead, you constantly suffer from things not being grabable or dropped by the katana. A special kind of pain: the doneness indicator UI doubled in eyes.

The best part by far was Time Run. It works purely because of level design you can feel the flow, and that was cool. Until I reached levels with bad level design.
In Endless, the level design is much worse: no interesting moments, no interesting transitions.
In Time Run you dont need to press a Start button before the run. In Endless this drives me crazy, because you have to walk up to it, and for some reason youre moving slowly while doing so.

Theres very little impact and UX feedback in general. You dont understand when you took damage, why the hook latched onto the wrong point, why you did two dashes instead of one, etc.

The cat well, its a cat. It purrs and meows, you can scratch things that part is nice.

There are only two enemy types (maybe more, but i dosent reach they), and they dont really feel like enemies, more like traps. They stand still and die in one hit.

Interactive objects on the levels mostly work poorly. The only exception is the zipline - its simple and, surprisingly, actually works. Red barrier is my pain, I dash on it, but he reflect me and I falled. It works correctly at ~30% times

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the feedback. 

While you mention a lot of bugs, none of them are described in a way that lets us reproduce or identify them precisely. If you’re open to it, concrete examples or short clips shared in our Discord would help us understand what exactly went wrong on your end.

Movement in Catana is indeed inspired by our previous games like STRIDE. Locomotion is a core pillar across all our projects and something many players come to us for. That said, Catana also introduces new runner-focused mechanics, such as destructible red barriers, hook-only blue walls, enemy shields, and obstacle-based flow changes that weren’t present before.

Regarding combat and dash: the dash is not automatic. It requires both correct timing and a physical hand motion, simply pressing the trigger isn’t enough. 

In the restaurant, if objects frequently stick to the katana, that’s usually related to blade calibration. The katana position can be adjusted in the settings, which often resolves those grab issues.

Endless mode is built around difficulty levels, modifiers, and procedural chunk generation, which is why its structure differs from Time Runs. Each run is meant to play differently depending on the modifiers you choose.

As for feedback and damage: when you take a hit, your HP bar is shown directly on the screen.

We understand Catana won’t work for everyone, but the systems you’re describing are intentional design choices rather than missing or unfinished features. If you’re willing to share more specific technical details, we’re always open to looking deeper.

reviews.review_title: Doesn’t feel like a cat ninja or parkour
reviews.review_description:
Cat mode is basically unplayable. I keep falling off because I would just slide off, jump too high over the enemy, and glitch through the walls. The dash attack is strange cuz you charge into the enemy and they look like they were sliced in half. However, you dont ever have the time to draw the katana. Alot of times the jump is too much and you just soar over the enemies and can’t land a strike. There are multiple levels but they get repetitive over time. When taking a break from the combat, you’re in this village of sorts, mostly a restaurant where you take orders and cook the correct food to never ending customers. If this is meant to be a chill relaxation after the sweaty running, it’s anything but relaxing. Not to mention not much other interactions / immersions besides a pretty old drunk man that pukes a lot. So you’re constantly sweating running or cooking food…I’m keeping the game in hopes future fixes improve the experience as it is still early release. The parkour off the walls and slicing the enemies in half is pretty cool but besides that, nothing else has really struck out to me that screams wow. 

One month later review: there hasn’t been any noticeable change since I played on launch day. The cat mode is still broken. I’m still phasing and getting stuck in walls, constantly falling off after landing, and can’t crouch when using the stick. Why can’t the crouch simply be assigned to the b button like majority of other games? Also, the jump is too sensitive that I end up soaring over the enemy which ruins the main attraction of this game which was slicing up the enemy. Yes I can jump and dash into them, but they prematurely slice apart upon the dash impact, not from sword. And if you stop to bloc bullets or turn around to fight them, the decay thing that kills you for not going fast enough gets you.The whole parkour thing doesn’t exist either. It’s not real parkour if you are limiting movement by only allowing controlled movements. Parkour is free movement not controlled by binary movements like well timed pressing a b or c. It should be acrimonious wide range of non binary movements. Just from my experience, I can’t recommend this game. I guess others somehow had a better experience but nothing about the controls in this game were intuitive.

My first experience with life as a cat and it does not disappoint.  The speedy parkour while (hopefully) collecting things and taking out dudes along the way with catana in hand...I'd have to say, keeps things busy in this realm.  It's like there is a space for calm and a space to go wild in this game.  I played this sitting and standing.  I recommend sitting if you lose balance easily, just to ease into it.  Be mindful that the cat meows can be heard outside the headset and might attract some feline attention from the real world one does not anticipate while slashing through this game Catana-style.

reviews.review_title: Great game with many different styles
reviews.review_description: Catana is a fun game where you can switch between parkour and running a restraint. The parkour/action part of this game is my personal favorite as in the movement is very swift and the combat is satisfying. The cooling part of the game is also fun where you have to buy and upgrade item that you can seek to different customers. Finally there is the other game modes like endless these game modes add even more repetition just for more reasons to come back to the game

The easy-to-learn controls are a major win, allowing you to intuitively wall-run, dash, and deflect bullets without the clunky learning curve often found in VR games. Whether you're chasing high scores in the time trials or managing a chaotic restaurant with Grandpa between missions, the game stays accessible and engaging throughout. It’s a must-play for anyone who wants a polished, adrenaline-fueled movement game that doesn't sacrifice fun for complexity.

This is a platforming game that fits nicely into the other Stride catalog of games. I appreciate the unique approach with the more cartoony style and the fact that your character is a cat. The main game is fun, although, I feel like it gets old really quick. The restaurant game mode is unque and also fun as well.

I played this game since a semi-early beta build, and I feel like the tycoon part is intended for a different audience. I dont deny that it still fits the setting of the game, but I dont think its for everyone.
I personally am not too invested in the restaurant part of the game, and Im far more into the runner/platformer part of the game. For a while, I held the top score until in the public release, and the way I got there was because there hasnt been much variation to the running part. Now that the full release is out theres a good amount now in it, and certainly adds to replayability, but like an endless anything, itll start to feel like a “been there, done that” feeling, just not as fast. 
The tycoon part I couldnt really get into because of some weird collision bugs, and is honestly not my pace, but those who are into it will certainly enjoy the mini-games.

This feels more like an upgrade for Original Stride than a new game. Its mostly the same with only a couple new mechanics, and better graphics. They did add a new hub, with mini-games and currency, but its really aimed more at children. Overall, you would either have to not own the original, or just really want new levels, in order to purchase this.

reviews.review_title: A simple but decent little game
reviews.review_description: It’s a simple game that’s basically in two parts. One part of it is a restaurant sim type game that’s quite simple for its type, but it’s fun enough to kill a few minutes when bored, and when you’re bored of that just bothering the customers is fun too. The other half is a game more similar to their previous works, though more speed and melee focused instead of gun combat. The dashes and sliding work amazingly for me, but I’ve personally found the wall run to be finicky, but that might be more my issue since I haven’t seen people complain about it. I hope that this game continues to see development since I feel there is potential here. It’s fun enough and is something to pick up if you’re bored and looking for something you can jump into now and again without a big time investment, fun simple and cheap little game.

Ever since I was wee little kitten, I weild the catana with grace. The enemies were no match to my superior fighting, parkour, and COOKING skills. Nothing was better then grilling up a fish after a tedious mission. I even decided to start my own kitchen.

          This game is very fun to play. I got focused into the game so many different times. I slowly but surely realized how to master the catana. I was very suprised to load up the game and see a resturant when I first played. The resturant is actually very fun to do.

        I 10/10 recommend getting this game. It is fun, strategic, and a bit of a workout. There might be games that compare, but they can't beat the feeling of knocking down an old man in a robe for 1200th time.

P.S.: The game was provided to me for free to do playtesting.

reviews.review_title: Good for letting off steam and relaxing
reviews.review_description:
Okay, I tried Katana: Red Flowers and frankly, it's the perfect stress reliever. You play as a ninja cat (yeah, the concept is already brilliant) and the goal is simply to slice and dice everything that moves.

The gameplay is incredibly fast-paced; you really get a sense of power as you rack up kills while wall-running. However, there's a slight downside: it's not the longest game in the world and it can get a little repetitive at times, but for the price, the adrenaline rush is definitely there. If you have 20 minutes to kill and want to feel like a samurai on steroids, go for it!

This game does its best to offer a ninja parkour experience while offering other game modes to keep you occupied. Race through the levels as a ninja cat in order to defeat enemies as fast as possible. Collect coins while you run, slide, grapple, and fight enemies in each level in order to unlock different cosmetics for your appearance.  The gameplay requires you to move your arms to run, so be prepared to get your heartrate elevated if you're trying to get to the end as fast as possible. Thankfully, you don't have to move your arms like a monkey in order to traverse around the levels. There are objectives to complete during levels in order to progress. There is also a restaurant mini game that somewhat feels out of place. It is nice to test out your culinary skills when taking a break from the main game, but it didn't keep me engaged. All in all, this is a fun parkour game mixed with ninja like components. It isn't too deep of a game, but it is always fun to parkour in VR.

Think of Geometry dash, but in 3D. Then add some extra levels and features. Then set it in Japan.

Love this game, especially endless mode. Highly addictive(in a good way). If you like Geometry dash. You will love this. Highly recommended.


I have been provided this game free of charge for the purpose of testing.

There a different modes to play. 

You can open a restaurant and serve dished for your customers. Just watch out that grandpa is not gonna throw up on your dishes. 
You can climb the leaderboard when preparing dishes. Love it.

There are also other mini games, that you can play in the restaurant when it is closed. But you have to unlock them first to play. Just collect points while playing endless or time mode.

It took some practice to know all the movements. I had to do the tutorial a few times.  But still fail sometimes on walljumpings.

But it is fun. 

If you want some action, you can do time mode or endless mode. If you want more relaxation, open your restaurant and serve some customers or play the mini games..

reviews.review_title: Stride meets animal crossing. But why?
reviews.review_description:
This is very similar to Stride as far as movement and mechanics are concerned, but with a more cartoonish take on the theme. 

If you were a fan of Stride, you’ll probably enjoy the main gameplay. I personally found it quite unforgiving and difficult to get into a flow (maybe skill issue).  I also wish there was more content for the price of the game. 

Parkour can be very fun in VR, but it honestly does feel a bit patched together and uninspired. It’s my kind of genre. I want to love it. But I struggled quite a bit to get any enjoyment out of this game. That being said, it might just not be for me. 

The minigame hub is also a fun idea, but I really struggled to see why it was there and how it connected to the rest of the game. The implementation felt flat. It feels like maybe it’s trying to reach two different audiences? The genres felt more disparate and shallow in a way that unfortunately made me dislike both parts, but again that is very subjective, and I do appreciate them trying to add some more interactive fun.

Full disclosure: a copy of this game was provided. 

Summary: If you are a fan of violent parkour games fused with a silly mini game hub, all while being a cat, then pounce on this game. However, if you are looking for a more serious, narrative-driven game, ￼you’ll find it in another cat tower. 

My thoughts: While this type of game ultimately turned out to not be my personal cup of tea, I can see other VR gamers looking forward to throwing on their headset and playing for 30-45 minutes at a time before moving on to the next thing. The parkour system has a little bit of a learning curve, but there is a handy tutorial that will help players practice the moves until they become second nature. Or if you are not a fan of the violence, you can stay in the mini-game hub world and make food for your animal guests, or beat them up, ￼whatever floats your goat. This game is not meant to be taken seriously and will be enjoyed by gamers who like being silly. ￼If that sounds like your kind of catnip then check it out.

reviews.review_title: If you like cats and ninjas, this is 4 U
reviews.review_description: Your a cat, and a ninja, best of both worlds. Requires good VR legs but is very fun with lots to do. I really enjoyed all the little mini games about making food. Help your poor owner out by kicking butt and cooking up a storm. meeeooow.

I was already a fan of stride, parkour and VR just mixes so well when you dont get motion sick. Ive been a junkie for the endless mode there so i was happy that they did the same here.
By the way, i am only talking about the endless mose of this game, i havent tried anything else but there is more.

Ive had so much fun. It just feels like a better version of stride since it has a faster pace. Dashing gives the movement alot more depth and allows for alot of chaining dashes which is really satisfying. Especially after i found out that wallrunning resets dashes aswell i could turn my movement a few gears up. The melee and its dashes really lend themselves to the parkour gameplay. compared to stride, where shooting felt like a distraction, the melee here is an extension to the parkour giving it a nice flow.
The only think i dont like that much is that i cant dash into the snipers which bullets i have deflected into. That is a point that breaks the flow a bit.

Catana is not a type of game that I would typically play in VR. So when I first started I was awful at it. The controls took a few runs for me to get accustomed to, but honestly the way the devs set them up makes sense for those looking for VR immersion. In this parkour style game, running through the time trials in the hand crafted levels was my preferred style of play. Searching for the secret paths to lower my time helped bring a small feeling of exploration and accomplishment. Personally I only spent enough time on the included restaurant mini-game to see that it was a chill environment to give players a break between runs. With all the parkour style movement of the main game those prone to motion sickness have a place to recoup in the restaurant while still earning some cosmetic upgrades. Overall the game was a fun ninja running experience.

reviews.review_title: Worse the original red flowers,dont buy!
reviews.review_description: Why couldnt you have just sticked with the original feel like its so catoony and boring now.

What can I say, it’s not a game for me however it may be for you. I will say there is to be fun to be had. If you like a bit of slashing mixed with some parkour and some mini games on the side it might be up your alley. 

Overall the game feels and runs just fine.

This was a fun and rather strange combo game that worked well in my opinion.  While the artstyle and music really complimented each other.  Not a huge fan of gtag style movements but didn't bother me so bad in this one. 
  You start off with a cooking game with lots to do and fun mini games around the kitchen.   Spent a great amount of time here building up reputation.  
  Then you move onto the the timed runs and endless mode.   Each  with a leader boards for the competitive players a few secret paths to find along the way.  There is a certain level of replay ability and with the cooking side keeps thing changing up to keep you entertained. However I can see how you can get board rather quickly. I believe more levels are a must.

I played the original red flowers demo and was eager to see the full release, when they announced it was a cat game I was skeptical at first but when I got hands on with the game I found that being a cat did not deviate from the overall ghost runner experience at all and the fact you can play all the original levels from the demo makes this game all worth it

I just started playing this game yesterday and I had a blast. You literally get to be a cat playing as a ninja. It’s close to. I am cat 2 but except you’re a ninja cat you literally can cook for people and even troll and if you wanna be violent, you can cook them yourself and plus if you’re feeling like you want to do a endless run or a time run you’re gonna have to get a little bit used to how things work and also if you know how to use the stuff it’s super fun to just run around as a ninja cat and fight enemies, even in the endless mode plus you can enable or turn on the Gore level and if you wanna run as a cat, there’s a settings for that but if you wanna run like a ninja, there’s a settings for that too. That’s why I think this game would win game awards plus it has everything in one for just $15.

This game is strange. It tries to blend ninja gameplay, restaurant management, and season it with a cat theme, and it just doesn't work together. 
But as their own separate aspects, they're solid. The parkour and sword gameplay is addicting, the visuals and level design is excellent, and the restaurant management has a lot of features and quirky-ness. 

It's got some bugs and issues that need polishing, and is sadly very different from the original vision (aside from the time trials). But is also smart enough to not be overly ambitious, unlike most of JoyWay's previous games.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for the feedback. If you noticed any bugs or rough edges, sharing them in our Discord would be super helpful.

reviews.review_description:
The movement feels good and the game can be fun in short bursts, but it never really comes together as a whole. It feels like it’s trying to be two different kinds of games at once, and neither side gets enough depth to really stand out.

The time trials are easily the highlight. Straightforward, linear stages where you just focus on moving efficiently and going as fast as possible. They’re fun to replay and getting gold is the most satisfying part of the game.

The other two modes make up most of the game, but neither really holds your attention. One is an endless score-chasing mode that gets repetitive fast, and the other is a light restaurant mode that’s shallow and lacks any challenge. Together, they feel more like padding than meaningful gameplay.

The game looks sharp and the core mechanics are solid, but the focus feels split. If the whole game had leaned into the time trial design, it could’ve been something special. As it is, it’s decent, just uneven.

I don’t really like how JoyWay is pandering to a younger audience. The original Red Flowers was much more stylish and cool. That said, the core idea is still there — the mechanics are just as polished, and the gameplay is still very engaging.

P.S. Developers, please don’t forget about Stride: Fates. It’s an amazing game and doesn’t deserve to be abandoned.

The main course, the parkour, freerunning and fighting off the enemies feels great, and is really satisfying to play through! 
Getting in the zone, playing well is veeery satisfying, and a great feeling overall!

I don't vibe with the restaurant minigame, It's nowhere near complex enough for me to enjoy it, and i had some issue like hard to find time trials. 

I definitely think it's worth a buy, but its novelty may wear off after a few hours, and it might feel too same, but still - it's great!

developer_response.body:
Really glad the freerunning, combat, and flow clicked for you!

Just to clarify one thing you mentioned: Time Runs can be accessed directly from the portal in the hub, so you don’t have to go through the restaurant side to reach them.

reviews.review_description:
I started testing Catana: Red Flowers last May. I didn't expect to like it much, but I thought I could objectively provide useful feedback. The first version I tested was pretty basic, but I did keep thinking about it after that round of testing was over. I'll skip the progression of the game and get to the point. I ended up really liking this game.

It's still basically an endless runner, which is why I didn't expect to like it, but it's VR. Everything is better in VR, right? This time it's true. In a regular endless runner you duck, jump, maybe turn. Press a button, repeat. In Catana, you move your arms to run. You physically duck. Your left hand is a grappling hook and your right has a sword. Ok, you do press a button to jump, but it's more complicated, more interesting and it becomes natural fairly quickly.

They've added more, too. If you want, you have a restaurant full of mini games to play. There are challenge levels, too, but I'm not actually very good. I experimented with those other parts to get the game's full experience, but the rogue-like endless runner is my favorite part.

I do still wish there was some story to the game. I like to have some sense of progression. My lack of skill keeps the rogue like sections short, which kind of solves my issue with rogue-likes. It does limit how much I play the game, though. I enjoy pulling it out from time to time and it's very fun, but there's a limit to how much I can play.

I strongly recommend it, which is saying something considering how I shouldn't like this game.

This fun little arcade runner game will give you hours of enjoyment as you run dash and slash through your way through multiple stages or through the endless run mode. Then reward yourself by opening your shop and playing a diner dash Sim game to relax when you aren't too busy taking down bad guys. Between the fun puzzle stages trying to run through as flawless as possible to the endless stage where you play a subway surfer style endless game this will have you being a cat ninja for hours of fun.

I have conflicted feelings about Catana: Red Flowers. It feels like two games squished together.

The first of those games is the HUB area, which has a bright, colourful, vibrant cartoony artstyle. It's a rooftop garden, with a set of restaurant mini-games. You're meant to play around. You can catch fish, and serve food (multiple, simple mini-games) to the customers who approach your restaurant hut. You can swipe them with your cat claws, climb the walls, and there's even an electric razor you can menace them with. This is clearly meant for kids.

The second part of the game is based upon the Red Flowers demo the studio put out a while back. It's a parkour runner/slasher. The artstyle is Cyberpunk-esque and more dark and gritty than the garden HUB (think: Ghostrunner). You run by moving your arms physically back and forth in a "run-like" motion, jump with a button, dash by slashing your sword, and wall-run by running at a wall and jumping onto it. You slash enemies as you go, and even deflect bullets. This is the part of the game more for adults. I actually mostly enjoyed this part of it! There are 11 short levels, most of which can be completed within a couple minutes or less (at most) if you're practiced enough. You are timed, and the game is clearly designed for people who want to speedrun and beat their time. There's an endless mode, with modifiers, too. I got stuck on a couple levels, having to replay them several times, and I feel these frustrating moments were due to issues in level design that need to be polished/reworked.

I actually enjoyed the more cutesy, *almost* lo-fi adjacent music in the hub area, as well as the edgy electronic music in the runner-slasher gameplay levels. 

Overall, this is a pretty short experience and I feel it's more tailored to kids. There is no real story, beyond a single cutscene, and it's fairly light on content. I did playtest the game, so I got to play it for free, and I wanted to note that. IMO the price is a bit high, given the content on offer. I'm sure there will be people who pickup the game on sale and enjoy it, though it's a bit of a mixed bag for me personally.

Kinda of a weird combination of a cute little cat but hop around town like a mad man and chop any and all bad guys in a furry of blood or flowers if you turn on the PG mode (you must have a good VR leg to do this, a lot of fast pace jumps).  When he is all done, I guess with all the money this cat earned through his adventure, he runs a restaurant.  It is a strange combination but fun regardless.

Catana is a fun but not too challenging game for most parts. There's a lot of minigames and little functions to sink time in when you need a break from the parcour. Generally however there's nothing making this game stand out or especially interesting. The controls were realised pretty nicely however are there not many mechanics that make this game feel especially meaningful.

reviews.review_title: We ran out of ideas final boss
reviews.review_description: The game is just like stride to the movement climbing and wall running I felt no new real features being pulled off while playing this that weren’t already in stride the restaurant mini game was kinda fun but got boring after a bit as there was no real leveling system with it and why is there gorilla tag movement it dosent even play well nor fit in the game at all just say you wanna be I am cat if you want a parkour game buy stride it’s by the same people and I think the same price

developer_response.body:
Appreciate you sharing your thoughts, especially so early after release.

Just to clarify a few things: Catana’s progression is tied to the restaurant side and Endless mode. Also, in Endless mode, you can use modifiers to increase difficulty and change how each run plays, while the restaurant has its own progression system that opens up over time.

Catana’s movement isn’t 100% similar to Gorilla Tag. We have our own system built around speed, timing, and control, and it tends to click more the longer you spend with it.

reviews.review_description: Awesome game for people who liked that one demo of a similar game. I believe there was red and catana in the name anyway. The game is great with awesome mechanics and gameplay. This game works for gorrila tag lovers and normal running lovers awesome mechanic.

reviews.review_title: Solid title that just needs more content
reviews.review_description:
Catana mixes two vastly different syles of games to result in a fun package.
On one hand you have the core game score attack game which is similar vein to Flat games Mirrors edge or Ghost Runner.

Courses that you parkour your way through using your katana to dash and kill enemie/deflect bullet
Whilstt you wall run and grapple your way to the end.
Ita very arcady in nature and will appeal to those who like like that bite sized challenge and compete with others for faster times.
Ehilst there arent too many stages there are secrets paths and corners on stages you havr ti find if you want to compete on the leaderboard

There is also an endless mode to challenge other on too as well as to earn currancy to unlock some cosmetics

On the flip side of the coin is the mini game centric resturant building.
This mode is a series of minigames with a touch of wackiness thats wouldnt be too out of place something like "I am bouncer" it "Drunken Bar Fight"

Its a strange hybrid of genres.
Whilst they may not be the best of its class in each genre; it still have a stong base for the developer to push out some updates (more levels in the main parkour mode is what I like to see)

Its great for younger players (just make sure you turn of gore in the options! If you dont like your kids to see blood)

developer_response.body: Really appreciate the detailed feedback. We’re already gathering impressions from players and will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_title: Awesome game, feel like a ninja!
reviews.review_description: I had a lot of fun with this game. Running and jumping from wall to wall and slicing up enemies is a lot of fun, I love that there are different movement options for different play styles. I've found myself doing numerous runs trying to get a faster time, and attacking enemies is so much fun! This is a charming game with lots of character.

This is a unique arcade parkour platforming game with two different combat/obstacle course modes and a chill mode.  Time trial you try for the fastest time in a course that stays the same so you can better your time, in endless mode which is by far my favorite mode is a bit rogue like, the course is proceduraly generated so it changes for each run but also has wall climbing, sliding, grappling, and fighting enemies with your katanas and you can get power up modifiers to change up the playstyles.  You have to move fast as the world around you is shrinking and your run ends when it reaches you.  The movements similar to this studios stride series is very fluid and fun.  The art style is cute but then the combat animations are brutal.
The game also has a cooking simulator in the town hub where you cook and serve specific foods the customer asks for it can get a bit hectic, kind of an odd addition to a fighting game.  Im not big on cooking sims but there is progression system in it and you can cook more difficult items and upgrade your restaurant it has some funny interactions.

The game is nice, enjoyable with a stylish design. The gameplay is well balanced, fast and addicting. There is also a restaurant section which takes the same idea of other games....maybe this could have been done in a different way to make the game more unique.the game has solid bases to become one of the top of the store

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you enjoyed the gameplay and style, and thanks for sharing your thoughts on how it could evolve.

reviews.review_title: Good action parkouring and some cooking
reviews.review_description:
Really nice feeling of the movement and attacks.
The time attack levels are great, just wish there were more.
Endless mode is fine but a bit repetitive.
Cooking part pretty fun but not the main reason to get the game.

I definitely do recommend this game to anybody. Having a slice them enemies down plus you get to be a cat. I definitely would love to see more content. This game has came along ways and it’s definitely worth playing. 
￼
I played red flowers demo back in 2022 and had a lot of fun with it. Tried the full game and yeah it slaps. The movement, speed, parkour are still the best for me.
If you enjoyed stride this will be a similar experience with a nice cartoon aesthetic and a nice cooking game on the side to just chill and earn currency.
Give them time to update the game and we will see the development

reviews.review_title: High-Speed Combat with a Cozy Twist
reviews.review_description:
The game surprised me in the best way. One minute I was dashing through enemies, deflecting bullets with my katana, feeling like a stylish ninja cat straight out of an anime. The combat clicked quickly, but mastering the speed and precision took real practice, which kept me coming back. The locomotion options are fantastic I switched between parkour and climbing depending on my mood, and both felt natural in VR.

Then Running a chaotic little restaurant as the same cat was hilarious and genuinely fun. Cooking, serving, and messing with impatient guests through mini-games added a cozy, playful contrast to the combat.

It’s not flawless, but the variety, personality, and replayability make Catana an easy recommendation, especially for VR players who enjoy fast action mixed with charm.

If you liked stride this game is for you. This game builds upon stride by adding more varied areas and more abilities the parkour and combat feel butter smooth and there’s plenty of side content to go through if you wanna take a break would recommend

Fun game fully realized and so colorful and immersion. Loved being a cat in vr, battling my way through levels, and finding out what's right around the corner. I found myself always coming back to get higher times, show this off to fellow cat lovers, and just to have a fun time. Would recommend to people if you are just looking for nothing serious and just want fun and good times. Nice job developers.
A nice balance of skill and speed, it stands out from other VR games with it's unique gameplay. I enjoyed it. Some frame drops on Meta Quest 2 but nothing deal breaking.

I Got This Game The Minute It Came Out And BOY I Was Chuffed, I Loved The Demo So The Real Game Is Even Better. 1 Thing, Can They Add Shifts To The Game? On The Cooking Part I Don’t Like Controlling When To Open And Close.

reviews.review_title: Day one of waiting till they update
reviews.review_description: Update Pls it’s been awhile
Watch out for your friends... They will try their best to outmaneuver you. A fun game to play!
Having fun right now
Run, slice and earn money
